paper_id: Porello et al. - 2020 - A Core Ontology for Economic Exchanges

models.model_label: Core OntoUML ontology of economic exchanges based on ATE (grounded in UFO)

evidence_quotes.quote: The goal of this paper is to use the ATE as a guide to lay down the main aspects of a core ontology of economic interactions.
In the subsequent sections, we shall develop an ontological account of economic transactions according to the ATE.

Two important issues regarding the nature of economic exchanges -which are quite pressing for a fruitful ontological understanding of economic interactions- are the nature of the things being exchanged and the matter of the agreement between the transacting agents.

goals.justification: The paper's explicit stated purpose is to clarify/conceptualize the nature of economic exchanges via a core ontology, matching the 'For Understanding' signals (understand, clarify, conceptualize, ontological understanding of the domain).

evidence_quotes.quote: To demonstrate the expressiveness and generality of our core ontology, we use it to analyze and ground both REA and UFO-S.
We confront now our OntoUML model of ATE with UFO-S.
evidence_quotes.section: 4 ATE at Work: Generalising REA and UFO-S

evidence_quotes.quote: Therefore, our modeling provides, on the one hand, a generalisation of REA to transactions involving any type of services and, on the other hand, an explanation of why the transaction occurs
evidence_quotes.section: 4 ATE at Work: Generalising REA and UFO-S

goals.justification: The authors use their model to analyze, evaluate, and compare existing standards (REA and UFO-S), identifying shortcomings (e.g., REA's partial perspective, lack of explanation) and showing how they can be generalized. This matches the 'analyze options', 'evaluate', 'identify shortcomings' signals for Problem-solving without constituting actual implementation.

evidence_quotes.quote: So to put the ATE to work in modeling applications, we present a reusable OntoUML model of economic exchanges.
To make our investigation precise and applicable to designing wellfounded Information Systems, we shall place our analysis within the Unified Foundational Ontology (UFO) [7-9].

uncertain_goals.reason: The model is described as 'reusable' and 'applicable to designing well-founded Information Systems', but no actual implementation, deployment, or generated artifact is reported; this is framed as potential future applicability rather than an executed intervention.
